Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint returns all products correctly
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains product data
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify response structure
4. Verify response contains product data
Expected Result: 1. Status code: 200\n2. Response is JSON array\n3. Each product has id
Tester: category\n4. Response contains valid product data

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint supports pagination correctly
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains multiple products
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?page=1&limit=5
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify response structure
4. Verify pagination metadata
5. Send GET request to /products?page=2&limit=5
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. Response contains pagination metadata
3. Page 1 returns first 5 products
4. Page 2 returns different products
5. Total count is accurate

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint filters by category correctly
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains products with categories
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?by_category=Tools
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify all returned products belong to Tools category
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. All products have category 'Tools'
3. Response contains only Tools category products
4. Response structure is correct

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint filters by brand correctly
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains products with brands
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?by_brand=DeWalt
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify all returned products belong to DeWalt brand
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. All products have brand 'DeWalt'
3. Response contains only DeWalt brand products
4. Response structure is correct

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint supports search functionality
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains products with various names
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?q=hammer
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify returned products contain 'hammer' in name or description
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. All returned products contain 'hammer' in name or description
3. Search is case-insensitive
4. Response structure is correct

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint filters by price range
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains products with various prices
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?min_price=10&max_price=100
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify all returned products have price between 10 and 100
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. All products have price >= 10 and <= 100
3. Price filtering works correctly
4. Response structure is correct

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint supports sorting by price
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains products with various prices
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?sort=price&order=asc
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify products are sorted by price in ascending order
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. Products are sorted by price ascending
3. First product has lowest price
4. Last product has highest price

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint handles invalid parameters gracefully
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products?page=invalid
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify error message is clear
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 422 or 400
2. Clear error message about invalid parameter
3. Response contains error details
4. API doesn't crash

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint works with authentication
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Valid authentication token available
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Verify response structure
4. Verify authentication doesn't affect public endpoint
Expected Result:
1. Status code: 200
2. Response structure is correct
3. Authentication doesn't break functionality
4. Public endpoint remains accessible

Test case name/Objective: Verify GET products endpoint responds within acceptable time
Precondition: 1. API server is running\n2. Database contains reasonable amount of data
Test steps:
1. Send GET request to /products
2. Measure response time
3. Verify response time is under 2 seconds
4. Verify response is complete
Expected Result:
1. Response time < 2 seconds
2. Status code: 200
3. Complete response received
4. No timeout errors
